ground_truth.title: What is a Mobile Application?
ground_truth.author: Bhaval Patel
ground_truth.main_content:
Mobile app refers to a software application developed for smartphones and tablets that offers various functions and services to users. Mobile apps allow consumers to perform myriad activities, retrieve any required data, and interact with services instantly. You can download such apps from different app shops like Apple's App Store and Google Play Store.

Native Apps: Native apps are custom-built for certain operating systems like iOS and Android. These native apps are famous for providing high performance and smooth integration with the hardware of the device. Native apps have access to device functions like the camera and push notifications to provide users with rich and immersive experiences.

Web Apps: Web applications are very practical because you can start using them on your mobile web browser immediately. These apps are easy to access and use compared to native apps, which you install on your device.

Hybrid Apps: Hybrid apps combine the best features of native and web apps. These apps are built with web technologies like HTML, CSS, and JavaScript yet are not enclosed within a native container.

Progressive Web Apps (PWAs): Progressive web apps are like pioneers in mobile technology, bridging the gaps between native and web apps. These apps provide the convenience of web access while also including functionality often associated with native apps.

Mobile apps consist of two parts: Front-end (client side) where users directly interact on their devices, and Back-end (server side) responsible for the logic of the application and handling data.
with: Mobile app refers to a software application developed for smartphones and tablets that offers various functions and services to users.
Web applications are very practical because you can start using them on your mobile web browser immediately.
